audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot renewed covenant text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for renewed tablets, YHWH character proclamation, covenant renewal, anti-idolatry commands, festivals, Moses' forty days, shining face.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses, Carve for yourself two tablets of stone like the first, and I will write upon tablets the words that were on first tablets, which you shattered.
Be ready for morning, and come up in morning to Mount Sinai; station yourself there for me on top of mountain.
No man shall come up with you, and also no man shall be seen in all mountain; even flock and herd shall not graze before that mountain.
Moses carved two tablets of stone like the first; he rose early in morning and went up to Mount Sinai, as YHWH commanded him, and took in his hand two tablets of stone.
YHWH descended in cloud and stationed himself with him there, and called in name YHWH.
YHWH passed before his face and called: YHWH, YHWH, Divine compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, abundant in loyal love and faithfulness/truth,

keeping loyal love to thousands, lifting/carrying iniquity, transgression, and sin/misalignment, yet clearing, he does not clear, visiting iniquity of fathers upon children and children's children to third and fourth.
Moses hurried, bowed low to earth, and worshiped.

He said, If now I have found favor in your eyes, my Lord, please let my Lord go in our midst, for it is stiff-necked people; and forgive/lift our iniquity and our sin/misalignment, and take us as inheritance.

He said, Look, I am cutting covenant. Before all your people I will do wonders that have not been created in all earth and in all nations; all the people in whose midst you are shall see work of YHWH, for it is fearsome what I am doing with you.
Keep for yourself what I command you today. Look, I am driving out from before you Amorite, Canaanite, Hittite, Perizzite, Hivite, and Jebusite.
Guard yourself lest you cut covenant with inhabitant of land where you are coming, lest it become snare in your midst.
But their altars you shall tear down, their standing stones shatter, and their Asherim cut down.
For you shall not bow to another god, for YHWH, whose name is Jealous, is jealous Divine.
Lest you cut covenant with inhabitant of land, and they prostitute after their gods and sacrifice to their gods, and one call to you and you eat from his sacrifice,
and you take from his daughters for your sons, and his daughters prostitute after their gods and make your sons prostitute after their gods.
Molten gods you shall not make for yourself.
Festival of Unleavened Bread you shall keep: seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at appointed time of month Aviv, for in month Aviv you went out from Egypt.
Every opener of womb is mine, and every male livestock, opener from ox and sheep.
Opener of donkey you shall redeem with lamb; if you do not redeem, break its neck. Every firstborn of your sons you shall redeem. None shall appear before my face empty.
Six days you shall work, and on seventh day cease; in plowing and harvest you shall cease.
Festival of Weeks you shall make for yourself, firstfruits of wheat harvest, and Festival of Ingathering at turning of year.
Three times in year all your males shall appear before face of Lord YHWH, Divine of Israel.
For I will dispossess nations from before you and broaden your border; no one shall covet your land when you go up to appear before face of YHWH your Divine three times in year.
You shall not slaughter blood/life of my sacrifice with leaven; sacrifice of Festival of Passover shall not remain overnight until morning.
Beginning of firstfruits of your ground you shall bring to house of YHWH your Divine. You shall not boil a kid in milk of its mother.
YHWH said to Moses, Write for yourself these words, for according to mouth of these words I cut covenant with you and with Israel.
He was there with YHWH forty days and forty nights; bread he did not eat and water he did not drink. He wrote upon tablets words of covenant, the ten words.

It happened when Moses descended from Mount Sinai, and two tablets of testimony were in Moses' hand as he descended from mountain, Moses did not know that skin of his face shone because he had spoken with him.
Aaron and all children of Israel saw Moses, and look, skin of his face shone, and they feared coming near him.
Moses called to them, and Aaron and all leaders in congregation returned to him, and Moses spoke to them.
Afterward all children of Israel came near, and he commanded them all that YHWH had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.
When Moses finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face.
When Moses came before YHWH to speak with him, he removed veil until he went out; then he went out and spoke to children of Israel what he was commanded.
Children of Israel saw face of Moses, that skin of Moses' face shone; Moses returned veil over his face until he went in to speak with him.

translation_notes: Exodus 34:6-7 is a central YHWH character proclamation: compassionate, gracious, slow to anger, abundant loyal love and faithfulness, yet not ignoring injustice.
Moses immediately applies the proclamation by asking YHWH to go in their midst and forgive/lift sin.
Renewed covenant repeats anti-idolatry, festivals, firstborn redemption, Sabbath, firstfruits, and Passover memory after calf rupture.
Land/dispossession language remains source-preserved and ethically flagged.
Moses' shining face from speaking with YHWH should be preserved; later veil reception history requires careful audit.
safety_note: YHWH's mercy proclamation must not erase accountability, and accountability must not erase mercy. Land and anti-idolatry commands must not be used to justify modern violence or dispossession.
